nodes.label: IdentityProvider port
nodes.rationale: Authentication becomes a swappable adapter in the hexagon: authorization_url(state) and exchange(code) -> ExternalIdentity(subject, email, groups). SSO only changes how identity is established; it reuses the existing JWT session layer, no second session system.

nodes.label: OIDCIdentityProvider adapter (Authlib)
nodes.rationale: Generic OIDC with discovery + PKCE + JWKS id_token verification; Google/Microsoft as config presets. OIDC chosen first over SAML because most SaaS IdPs speak OIDC.

nodes.label: Authlib (chosen over fastapi-sso)
nodes.rationale: fastapi-sso rejected after spikes: its OpenID result has no groups claim and it is FastAPI/Starlette-Request-coupled, awkward under Litestar. Authlib is framework-agnostic (httpx), installs on Python 3.14, and exposes full id_token claims including groups.

nodes.label: LiteLLM ui_sso.py (design inspiration, MIT)
nodes.rationale: Patterns ported, no code copied: provider selection by config, role derived from IdP groups with highest-privilege-wins, JIT provisioning, team membership reconciliation. Nothing from LiteLLM's proprietary enterprise/ was used.

nodes.label: JIT User Provisioning on SSO login
nodes.rationale: Create/update the SSO user (no password) on first login, bound to IdP sub with verified email required; support JIT and layer SCIM on top for deprovisioning.

nodes.label: SCIM 2.0 Provisioning/Deprovisioning
nodes.rationale: Deactivation must disable login and bump token_version to revoke sessions; SCIM auth uses a dedicated provisioning token separate from user JWTs and lsk_ keys.

nodes.label: IdP Group to Team/Role Mapping
nodes.rationale: Memberships reconciled on every SSO login so access follows the IdP as source of truth; kept a pure function for unit testability, with a manual-override escape hatch for platform admins.

nodes.label: Extended RBAC (roles to permissions)
nodes.rationale: Permissions stay enforced in the services (API is the source of truth; SSO/SCIM only populate identity), declarative and centrally checked, not scattered.

nodes.label: Enterprise Audit Log (planned)
nodes.rationale: Append-only record of privileged actions written off the hot path like the trace sink / usage events; failures must not break the request.
